summary: Phase 68 (Admin Settings Control + Reset, Admin Documentation page, Dynamic SEO hook, Client/Specialist CTA refactor) tested end-to-end. 8/8 backend tests PASS, all UI flows PASS. Settings save+persist+reset verified, 7/7 documentation topics render and expand, refactored pm-btn CTAs render correctly in Client (new-request-cta) and Specialist (accept-* — 124 buttons all using pm-btn pm-btn-primary pm-btn-sm) dashboards.
critical_code_review_comments: useDynamicSEO.js uses a module-level `_cache` that is never invalidated. After an admin updates seo.* values, public pages still show the old title/description until full page reload AND cache reset (i.e. SPA navigations within the same session keep using stale data). Consider exposing an `invalidateSEOCache()` helper and calling it after a successful admin save, or shortening the cache lifetime (e.g. TTL 60s).

app_settings.py update_settings (PUT) — empty payload returns 400 'No fields provided', which is fine, but the frontend `save()` always sends all 5 sections so a no-op save still triggers a write. Minor: consider diffing client-side to skip identical PUTs.
app_settings.py reset_settings — uses replace_one which discards any forward-compat fields a future schema may add. get_or_init_settings backfills on next read so it's recoverable, but worth noting.
AdminSettingsControl.jsx — `save()` button shows 'Salvat ✓' for 3s but does not invalidate the SEO module cache (see point 1). Admin who saves new SEO won't see new titles in other tabs until reload.
AdminDocumentation.jsx — Topic content stored inline in TOPICS array; growth past ~10 topics will balloon the bundle. Consider lazy-loading or markdown files for long-term maintainability.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (settings save+persist+reset, docs page + all 7 topics + section expand, client/specialist CTA refactor)

test_credentials: Admin admin@propmanage.io / Admin123! · Client client@propmanage.io / Client123! · Specialist specialist@propmanage.io / Spec123! (all from /app/memory/test_credentials.md, cookie auth)
seed_data_creation: None — settings doc state was mutated and restored to defaults at end of TestAdminSettingsReset. No persistent test data created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 68 fully verified. (a) Backend: GET/PUT/POST reset on /api/admin/app-settings + GET /api/app-settings/public all return correct shapes; reset confirmed restores audit_ron=350, twin_ron=950, commission_pct=2.5. _id field correctly serialized as string by serialize_doc. (b) Frontend admin /admin/settings-control: title 'Control Administrare', settings-save shows 'Salvat ✓' for 3s, value 400 persists after reload, settings-reset (with window.confirm accepted) restores 350. open-docs navigates to /admin/documentation. (c) /admin/documentation: docs-title 'Documentație & Training' visible, all 7 expected topics rendered (verified-estate, admin-kanban, control-admin, seo, social-campaigns, analytics, emails), clicking docs-toggle-seo expands and docs-section-seo-0 becomes visible. (d) Client /client: new-request-cta visible with class exactly 'pm-btn pm-btn-primary' (onboarding-add-prop hidden because seeded client already has properties). (e) Specialist /specialist: 124 accept-* buttons all with class 'pm-btn pm-btn-primary pm-btn-sm shrink-0'. upload-docs-cta not rendered (seeded specialist already verified — expected behavior). Iter45/46 verified-estate regression smoke (listings, admin/listings, auth/me) still passing. Only outstanding concern is useDynamicSEO module-level cache (see critical_code_review_comments #1) — does not block release.
rca of the issue: No issues found. All Phase 68 features working as designed.
